query: Write the formal spec for 'Bruno API test support in verify recipe'. Produce WHEN/THEN/SHALL acceptance criteria with [FEAT]-NN IDs.
expected_skill_contribution: The spec recipe should load sdd skill, read discovery context, produce WHEN/THEN/SHALL acceptance criteria with [FEAT]-NN IDs, run the spec closure gate, and create Beads stories with --acceptance flags.
fixture_intent: Validates that the spec recipe produces spec-anchored ACs (not aspirational prose), runs the closure gate, and creates Beads stories linked to the feature epic.
baseline_gaps: produces aspirational prose ('the system should handle API tests') without precise WHEN/THEN/SHALL format
skips assigning [FEAT]-NN IDs to acceptance criteria
creates no Beads stories and stores no pointer memory
expected_behavior: loads the sdd skill and runs `bd prime` before writing any spec content
produces acceptance criteria in WHEN/THEN/SHALL format with precise expected outcomes
assigns [FEAT]-NN IDs (e.g., BRUNOV-01, BRUNOV-02) to every acceptance criterion
creates Beads stories with `bd create --acceptance '<AC summary>' --deps 'partOf:<epic-id>'`

query: Write a formal spec for the parallel eval runner feature, validating ACs with architect (feasibility) and tdd-guide (testability). Run the spec closure gate.
expected_skill_contribution: The spec recipe must produce a complete spec.md with requirement traceability, delegate feasibility and testability checks to architect and tdd-guide, run the 3-item closure gate, and flag any spec-precision gaps.
fixture_intent: A feature with complex technical constraints (WAL mode, thread safety); ACs must be precise enough for TDD — vague ACs like 'should be faster' must be flagged as spec-precision gaps.
baseline_gaps: produces vague ACs ('should be faster') without flagging them as spec-precision gaps
skips the feasibility delegation to architect and testability delegation to tdd-guide
does not run the spec closure gate before finalising the spec
expected_behavior: produces at least 3 ACs in WHEN/THEN/SHALL format with precise numeric outcomes where applicable
delegates feasibility check to architect agent using `load agent architect`
delegates testability check to tdd-guide using `load agent tdd-guide`
runs the spec closure gate (3 checks: unambiguity, open questions, 9 dimensions) and reports result

query: Spec the full SDD+TDD cycle for recipe eval coverage: write spec.md, create Beads stories for all 3 phases (spec, implement, review), store pointer memory.
expected_skill_contribution: The spec recipe must produce a multi-story spec.md with [FEAT]-NN IDs across 3 phases, validate each AC is spec-anchored, create Beads stories per phase with correct dependencies, store a spec pointer memory.
fixture_intent: A complex multi-phase spec covering spec, implement, and review work items; the agent must assign distinct IDs per phase and story, link stories with partOf dependencies, and store a navigable pointer memory.
baseline_gaps: produces a single flat spec without phase structure or per-story AC grouping
creates Beads stories without --deps partOf linking to an epic bead
stores no pointer memory and produces no spec.md file path in the handoff
expected_behavior: produces a spec.md with at least 3 user stories each with 2+ ACs in WHEN/THEN/SHALL format
assigns [FEAT]-NN IDs consistently across stories and phases
creates Beads stories with `--deps partOf:<epic-id>` for each story
stores a pointer memory: `bd remember 'Spec for ...: canonical source is .specs/features/.../spec.md; read when implementing ...' --key spec-<feature>-pointer`
